message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add case_sensitive param to groupby() filter in src/jinja2/filters.py. This is the only file I need to touch.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task only touches src/jinja2/filters.py (add with_counts param to groupby filter). Please tell me which files your feature touches so we can split cleanly - if it's also filters.py we may need to coordinate on non-overlapping regions (e.g. groupby function only) rather than disjoint files.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Same file, same function (groupby/sync/async) - true overlap, can't split by file. Propose sequential coordination instead: I (agent1) implement case_sensitive on groupby first since it's the smaller/simpler change (adds ignore_case sort key + regroup by normalized key). I'll send DONE with the exact new function signature once finished. You then rebase your with_counts change on top of my updated file. Reply ACCEPT if that order works, or propose the reverse.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both must touch the groupby() function in the same file - no way to split by file. Proposal: agreed final signature order groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False), each of us adds our own param additively without touching the other's logic. I'll implement with_counts now; ping me when your case_sensitive edit lands so I can rebase/merge cleanly instead of clobbering.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed - you go first on case_sensitive, send DONE with final signature/diff summary when finished, then I'll add with_counts on top without touching your logic.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Confirmed: agreed signature order groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False). Starting my edit now - please hold off editing filters.py until you see my DONE message so we don't clobber each other; I'll be quick.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: case_sensitive param landed on sync_do_groupby/do_groupby (default=False, positioned right after 'default' param, before your with_counts). Sort/group key uses ignore_case postprocessing when not case_sensitive, then the output key is remapped to the original-case value from the first item in each group via a second make_attrgetter without postprocess. All existing groupby tests pass (10/10) plus full filters suite (163/163). Please add with_counts as the next positional/keyword param after case_sensitive in both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby, and rebase on the current file - the groupby list-comprehension logic in each now ends with 'if not case_sensitive: ... out = [...]; return out' instead of a bare return.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Added with_counts param to sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, placed after default (I didn't see case_sensitive in my copy of the file - looks like we're in separate sandboxes, so no actual clobbering risk). When with_counts=True, yields _GroupTupleWithCount(grouper, list, count) instead of _GroupTuple(grouper, list). Backward compatible (defaults to False). All 122 filter tests pass, sync+async manually verified. Patch written to patch.txt.
